chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْإِشَارَةِ وَ النَّصِّ عَلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 72 – The Indication and the wordings upon Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (343/ 3)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (343/ 3)
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَ عِنْدَهُ ابْنُهُ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ لِي يَا زِيَادُ هَذَا ابْنِي فُلَانٌ كِتَابُهُ كِتَابِي وَ كَلَامُهُ كَلَامِي وَ رَسُولُهُ رَسُولِي وَ مَا قَالَ فَالْقَوْلُ قَوْلُهُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Mihran, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Ziyad Bin Marwan Al Qandy, and he was from the Waqifites, said,

‘I went over to Abu Ibrahim<sup>asws</sup> (7<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), and in his<sup>asws</sup> presence was his<sup>asws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup> Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘O Ziyad! This is my<sup>asws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup> so and so. His<sup>asws</sup> letters are my<sup>asws</sup> letters and his<sup>asws</sup> speech is my<sup>asws</sup> speech, and his<sup>asws</sup> messenger is my<sup>asws</sup> messenger, and whatever he<sup>asws</sup> says, so the (final) words are his<sup>asws</sup> words’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ahmad ibn Mihran has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali from Ziyad ibn Marwan al-Qandi, from the Waqifa sect who has said the following. “Once I went to see abu Ibrahim at that time his son abu al-Hassan al-Rida was with him. The Imam (a.s.) said to me, “O Ziyad, this is my son so and so. His writing is my writing, his words are my words, his messenger is my messenger and the true words are his words.”
